lane_note_count: In lane {selected_lane_label}, how many note objects are there?
Look only at lane {selected_lane_label}. {note_object_rule_text} Count the visible note objects.
For lane {selected_lane_label}, report the number of note objects.
{note_object_rule_text} How many note objects are in lane {selected_lane_label}?
Count all visible note objects in lane {selected_lane_label}.
lane_note_score_value: Only lane {selected_lane_label} matters. For each note object in that lane, add the number shown for its color in the side POINTS palette. A tall note counts once. What is the total score?
Look only at lane {selected_lane_label}. Each note object scores the number printed for its color in the side POINTS palette. A tall note counts once. What is the total score?
Use the side POINTS palette. Add the color value for each note object in lane {selected_lane_label}; a tall note still counts once. What total do you get?
Only consider lane {selected_lane_label}. For each visible note object in that lane, use the side POINTS palette value for the note's color. A tall note counts once. What is the sum?
What is the total score for lane {selected_lane_label}? Add the side POINTS palette value for each note object in that lane. A tall note counts once.
most_notes_lane_label: Which lane number has the most note objects?
Across all lanes, count visible note objects. Which lane number has the largest count?
{note_object_rule_text} Report the lane number with the most note objects.
Which lane contains the highest number of visible note objects?
Identify the lane number with the most note objects.
earliest_hit_lane_label: {rhythm_motion_rule_text} Which lane number has the note that reaches the hit line earliest within {beat_window} beats?
Across all lanes, find the note closest to the hit line within {beat_window} beats. What lane number is it in?
{rhythm_motion_rule_text} Report the lane number of the first note to arrive at the hit line.
Which lane contains the note whose bottom edge is closest to the hit line, considering only the next {beat_window} beats?
{rhythm_motion_rule_text} Identify the lane number with the earliest arriving note.

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_falling_notes: a rhythm-game lane grid with colored falling notes, lane numbers, and a bottom hit line
code_prompt_defaults.rhythm_motion_rule_text: Notes fall one row per beat. The bottom line is the hit line. A long vertical note counts as one note.
code_prompt_defaults.note_object_rule_text: A long vertical note counts as one note object.
code_prompt_defaults.score_palette_rule_text: Use the side POINTS palette: each note scores the number shown for its color, and a tall note counts once.
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_lane_note_count: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] around all note objects in the specified lane
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_lane_note_score_value: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] around every note object in the specified lane that is included in the score
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_lane_note_score_value: set "answer" to the total integer score
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_most_notes_lane_label: set "annotation" to bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] around all note objects in the lane with the most note objects
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_most_notes_lane_label: set "answer" to the winning lane number as an integer
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_earliest_hit_lane_label: set "annotation" to the bounding box [x0, y0, x1, y1] around the note that reaches the hit line earliest
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_earliest_hit_lane_label: set "answer" to the winning lane number as an integer
